title: Chapter 10: The Data Haul
content: Mia cracked the admin server at 2:47 AM on day twenty-one.
Chloe was asleep when Mia shook her awake. The girl's face was pale in the dark, but her eyes were bright.
"I am in," Mia whispered. "The hidden server. I have full access."
Chloe was awake instantly. "Show me."
They moved to the bathroom. Mia opened the ceiling panel, pulled down the router, and connected her tablet. The screen glowed with a command-line interface that Chloe did not recognize.
"What am I looking at?"
"The server's root directory. Everything is here. Patient files. Financial records. Email archives. And something I did not expect."
"What?"
Mia opened a folder labeled *CORRESPONDENCE*.
"Every email between Dr. Webber and the families who send patients here. Including your father."
Chloe stared at the screen. "Show me the emails."
Mia opened the most recent one.

*From: Richard Hart*
*To: Marcus Webber*
*Subject: Update*
*Marcus,*

*Thank you for the weekly reports on Chloe's progress. We are pleased to see she has reached Yellow Tier. Please continue the current approach. We are confident the program will help her develop healthier priorities.*
*The quarterly payment for her care has been wired to your account. Please confirm receipt.*

*Best,*
*Richard*
Chloe read it twice. Her father was paying for weekly reports on her. Like she was an investment portfolio.

"There is more," Mia said. "The financial files show that Webber has been billing families 40,000 dollars per patient. But the facility's operating costs are 12,000 per patient. The difference goes to offshore accounts."
"How much?"
"Over the last three years? Approximately 4.2 million dollars."
Chloe leaned against the wall. She felt light. Not dizzy — light, like she was finally seeing the full picture. The facility was a business. The patients were inventory. The diagnoses were invoices.
"Download everything," she said.
"There is a problem. The file is too large to send over our router. We need physical access to the server to copy the data."
"Where is the server?"
"Basement. Room B-18. But the door is locked with a biometric scanner."
"Biometric?"
"Fingerprint. Only Webber and Nurse Cross have access."
Chloe looked at the camera in the corner of the bathroom. Then she looked at Mia.
"Then we need to get their fingerprints."
"How do you propose we do that?"
Chloe smiled. "We have a senator's daughter with a phone, a hacker with a tablet, and a nurse who touches everything with her bare hands. I have an idea."
The next morning, Chloe put the plan into motion.
Phase one: fingerprint acquisition.
At breakfast, she deliberately knocked over her water glass. It spilled across the table, soaking her tray and Tiffany's. She jumped up, apologizing, dabbing at the mess with a napkin.
Nurse Cross, as predicted, came over to supervise the cleanup.
"Careful, Chloe. Accidents happen when you are not paying attention."
"I know. I am so sorry. Let me wipe it up."
Chloe grabbed a stack of paper towels from the counter. But before she started cleaning, she paused.
"Nurse Cross? Could you hold this for a second?" She held out a paper towel. "I need to get more."
Nurse Cross took the paper towel out of habit.
Chloe smiled.
The paper towel had a thin layer of clear tape pressed onto the surface. Where Nurse Cross had gripped it, her fingerprints were now preserved on the adhesive.
Phase one: complete.
At 11:00 AM, Chloe found Amara in the third-floor common room.
"I need your phone," Chloe said.
"For?"
"To scan a fingerprint and create a silicone replica."
Amara stared at her. "You can do that with a phone?"
"I can do that with a phone and toothpaste. The phone scans the print. The toothpaste fills the ridges. You press it onto a biometric scanner and it reads the same as the real finger."
"That is terrifying."
"That is MacGyver-level problem solving."
Amara handed over her phone. Chloe photographed the paper towel with the fingerprint under the bathroom's harsh light.
She sent the photo to Mia.
*"Fingerprint acquired. Can you process this?"*
Mia's reply came thirty seconds later:
*"Give me two hours."*
At 3:00 PM, Chloe stood outside the basement door with a small piece of tape containing Nurse Cross's fingerprint.
Mia was on the tablet, connected to the router, monitoring the server.
"Ready?" Mia asked through the earbud Chloe had smuggled from the supply closet.
"Ready."
Chloe pressed the tape against the biometric scanner.
The light turned green.
The door clicked open.
The basement server room was small and cold. A single rack of servers hummed in the corner, their indicator lights blinking in arrhythmic patterns. The air smelled of ozone and dust.
Chloe plugged in the USB drive Amara had smuggled in.
The server accepted it without complaint.
"Copying now," Mia said. "The full financial database. All patient records. Email archives. Everything."
"How long?"
"Seven minutes."
Chloe stood in the dark and watched the progress bar crawl across the server's display.
Seven minutes.
That was all that stood between her and the evidence she needed to destroy this place.
The USB drive clicked. The transfer was complete.
Chloe pulled it out, slipped it into her pocket, and walked back through the basement corridor.
The biometric scanner locked the door behind her.
On the other side of that door, terabytes of incriminating data sat in the server racks.
On her side of the door, a small USB drive held everything she needed.
She touched her pocket to make sure it was still there.
It was.
She had the evidence.
Now she just needed to get it out.
That night, Chloe, Mia, Tiffany, Amara, and Sarah gathered in the bathroom — the only room without cameras.
Chloe held up the USB drive.
"This is everything. Financial records. Patient files. The emails between Webber and every family who has paid to put someone here."
"What do we do with it?" Tiffany asked.
"We send it to my college roommate, Priya. She is a paralegal. She will know what to do with it."
"How do we send it? The facility blocks all outgoing data."
Chloe looked at Amara. "You still have your phone. Does it have internet?"
"Limited. The facility blocks most sites. But I can access email."
"Can you attach a file?"
"Not at this size. The phone's data cap is too small."
"Then we need a different plan."
Mia spoke up. "I can compress the data into smaller encrypted chunks. We send them one at a time over several days. No single transmission will be large enough to trigger monitoring."
"How long will that take?"
"Five days."
Chloe looked around the circle of faces. Five women. Five different reasons for being here. One shared goal.
"Five days. Then we bring this place down."

---
Chloe sat on her bed that night and held the USB drive in her palm.
It was smaller than her thumb. But it contained enough evidence to destroy Serenity Peak.
To send Dr. Webber to prison. To expose her father.
To free every woman in this building.

She wrapped it in a sock and tucked it into the hollow floorboard.
Then she lay down and closed her eyes.
For the first time in weeks, she slept without dreaming of the gates.
